data.title: As a user, the onboarding session requires no more than 6 tool-use approval prompts, grouped into named batch scripts
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Audit Phase 3 Completion and restructure all setup operations into at most 6 named grouped batch scripts: (1) dep install, (2) scaffold .claude/ structure, (3) config write, (4) initial commit, (5) hook install, (6) final commit. Remove all ad-hoc individual tool-use approvals within each group. When a group has no actions (already resolved by confidence context), its prompt is skipped.

**Why**: SC4. Non-technical users are overwhelmed by repeated tool-use approval dialogs they don't understand. Named groups with a cap makes the flow comprehensible and matches the acceptance criterion.

**Scope**:
- IN: restructure Phase 3 into at most 6 grouped batch scripts with the named groups above, remove mid-group approvals, each approval prompt names its group
- OUT: content of what each script does (hook sequencing is S5's domain), question routing (S3)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, a fresh-repo onboarding session produces at most 6 Claude Code tool-use approval prompts, corresponding to the 6 named script groups
  <- Satisfies: "the user receives no more than 6 Claude Code tool-use approval prompts, corresponding to exactly these grouped script invocations"
- When this story is complete, no approval prompt appears outside these named groups during a fresh-repo run
  <- Satisfies: "From first prompt to final commit, the user receives no more than 6 Claude Code tool-use approval prompts"
- When this story is complete, each approval prompt names the group being approved (e.g., "Approve: scaffold .claude/ structure")
  <- Satisfies: "A reviewer can verify this by replaying a fresh-repo onboarding session and counting tool-use approval dialogs"
- When this story is complete, when a batch group has no actions to perform (all items already resolved by the confidence context), its approval prompt is skipped and the total count decreases accordingly
  <- Satisfies: "no more than 6 Claude Code tool-use approval prompts"

## Considerations

- [Reliability] S3's routing may reduce actions needed in the config-write group; the batch structure must handle empty groups gracefully without producing an empty approval dialog
- [Testing] Verify group count by replaying a fresh-repo onboarding session; scripted fixture should count tool-use approval events

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate when blocked — proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
